subject: Mathematics
title: Linear Equations
description: Master solving equations with one variable

explanation.definition: An equation where the variable has an exponent of 1, like x + 3 = 7

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: A linear equation is an algebraic equation in which each term is either a constant or the product of a constant and a single variable. When graphed, it forms a straight line.

sections.heading: Why Do We Care?
sections.content: Linear equations are everywhere! From calculating the total cost of groceries to predicting how much money you'll save over time, they help us model real-world relationships where things change at a constant rate.

worked_examples.explanation: We subtract 3 from both sides to isolate x

practice_problems.question: x + 5 = 12
hints: What do you add to both sides?

explanation.definition: Equations that require two steps to solve, like 2x + 4 = 10

sections.heading: The Two-Step Process
sections.content: First, handle the addition or subtraction to isolate the term with the variable. Second, handle the multiplication or division to solve for the variable.

explanation.definition: Equations with variables on both sides or parentheses

sections.heading: Brackets and Groups
sections.content: Use the distributive law to remove brackets first.

sections.heading: Clearing Fractions
sections.content: Multiply every term by the Lowest Common Denominator (LCD) to eliminate fractions.
